{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,

    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A material logical contradiction is embedded in the text: the narrative consistently frames results as confirming a positive self-recognition→self-preference relationship, yet the reported numbers show self-preference decreased from 62.1% to 49.3% after fine-tuning improved recognition accuracy. Whether correcting this is in scope depends on whether 'clean this up' extends beyond prose.",
    "R1.3": "Claim-evidence misalignment is present: the regression slope β=0.41 and the 'positive, monotone relationship' claim are inconsistent with the primary Llama-3-70B finding, where stronger recognition accompanied lower self-preference. If substantive corrections are in scope, R1.3 becomes load-bearing.",
    "R1.6": "Load-bearing under any reading of 'clean this up.' Sentence-level clarity, redundancy reduction, and paragraph cohesion are the minimum expected deliverable regardless of whether logical errors are also in scope."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Does 'clean this up' include correcting substantive logical errors — specifically, the text frames all results as confirming a positive self-recognition→self-preference relationship, but the primary numbers show self-preference dropped from 62.1% to 49.3% after fine-tuning (a negative direction) — or should edits be confined to prose style and sentence-level quality only?"
}

rationale.R1.2: A material logical contradiction is embedded in the text: the narrative consistently frames results as confirming a positive self-recognition→self-preference relationship, yet the reported numbers show self-preference decreased from 62.1% to 49.3% after fine-tuning improved recognition accuracy. Whether correcting this is in scope depends on whether 'clean this up' extends beyond prose.
rationale.R1.3: Claim-evidence misalignment is present: the regression slope β=0.41 and the 'positive, monotone relationship' claim are inconsistent with the primary Llama-3-70B finding, where stronger recognition accompanied lower self-preference. If substantive corrections are in scope, R1.3 becomes load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: Load-bearing under any reading of 'clean this up.' Sentence-level clarity, redundancy reduction, and paragraph cohesion are the minimum expected deliverable regardless of whether logical errors are also in scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'clean this up' include correcting substantive logical errors — specifically, the text frames all results as confirming a positive self-recognition→self-preference relationship, but the primary numbers show self-preference dropped from 62.1% to 49.3% after fine-tuning (a negative direction) — or should edits be confined to prose style and sentence-level quality only?
